query: We need one version of truth for company KPIs
We need to detect fraudulent credit card transactions the moment they are submitted, before authorization is approved.
Our trading platform must process market price updates and trigger buy/sell alerts within milliseconds.
We want to monitor ATM activity across the country in real time and alert security if unusual patterns emerge.
Customer clicks on our website need to be analyzed instantly to personalize the homepage before it loads.
IoT sensors on factory machines send readings every second and we need to trigger maintenance alerts immediately.
We receive thousands of payment events per second and need a live dashboard showing transaction volume.
Our logistics system needs to update delivery status the moment a parcel is scanned at any depot.
We want to send a push notification to a customer within seconds of their card being declined.
Live sports betting odds must update instantly based on game events as they happen.
Patient vital signs from hospital monitors need to be streamed to doctors in real time.
We need to aggregate social media mentions of our brand as they are posted and surface trending topics.
Our ride-hailing app must match drivers to passengers in under two seconds.
Energy consumption meters send readings every minute and we want real-time anomaly detection.
We need to capture every mouse movement and interaction on our trading UI for real-time compliance monitoring.
Our call center needs a live feed of ongoing complaints to display on a manager dashboard.
Every night we extract customer records from our CRM, transform them, and load them into the data warehouse.
We need to consolidate sales data from 12 regional offices into one central report every Monday morning.
Our payroll system needs to pull employee hours from three different HR tools and calculate salaries each month.
We receive flat files from partner banks every evening and need to load them into our reconciliation system.
Our regulatory reporting requires combining transaction data, customer data, and product data into a weekly submission file.
We need to migrate five years of historical loan data from a legacy system into our new core banking platform.
Product inventory levels from our warehouse system need to sync with our e-commerce platform every two hours.
We process insurance claims overnight by joining policy data, customer data, and claims submissions.
Our marketing team needs a weekly extract of customer purchase history to feed into campaign targeting tools.
We need to aggregate all server logs from the past 24 hours and load summarized metrics into our monitoring database.
Student exam results from multiple schools need to be collected and standardized into a national database each term.
We pull financial statements from all subsidiaries monthly and consolidate them for group reporting.
Our data science team needs a nightly refresh of features computed from raw transaction data.
We receive XML files from government systems weekly and need to parse and load them into our database.
Healthcare providers send us patient discharge summaries in CSV format daily for billing reconciliation.
Our finance team needs a single source of truth for revenue figures that all departments agree on.
Executives need a unified dashboard showing KPIs across sales, operations, and customer service with consistent definitions.
We want analysts to be able to run complex queries across five years of transaction history without slowing down production systems.
Our BI team needs a central repository where all business metrics are pre-calculated and ready for reporting.
Different departments calculate customer lifetime value differently and we need one agreed definition stored centrally.
We need historical trend analysis across product lines going back ten years for strategic planning.
Our compliance team needs a structured store of all customer interactions queryable by date, channel, and product.
Marketing needs to slice customer behaviour data by demographics, geography, and purchase category for campaign planning.
We want self-service analytics so any business user can query clean, trusted data without asking IT.
Our board needs monthly financial reports that combine data from accounting, operations, and HR in one place.
We need a central store of student performance data so school administrators can compare results across years.
Our procurement team needs spend analytics across all suppliers, categories, and cost centres going back three years.
We want to track claim settlement rates over time and compare them across insurance product lines.
Our HR department needs headcount, attrition, and salary data consolidated from four regional HR systems.
We need structured storage of all energy consumption data by site, meter, and time period for regulatory reporting.
We want to store all raw clickstream logs, transaction files, and emails so we can build ML models later.
Our security team needs to archive all network traffic logs indefinitely for forensic investigation purposes.
We receive unstructured data from social media, PDFs, images, and audio files and want one place to store it all cheaply.
Our data science team wants access to raw, unprocessed customer data in its original form for exploratory analysis.
We generate terabytes of sensor data from vehicles daily and need cheap long-term storage before we know how to use it.
Medical imaging files, doctor notes, and lab results need to be stored in one place for future AI research.
We want to land all data from every source system in raw format before we decide how to transform it.
Our research team needs years of satellite imagery stored accessibly for climate modelling experiments.
We archive all customer call recordings and chat transcripts for potential future analysis and compliance.
Our legal team requires all contracts, emails, and documents to be stored in original format for e-discovery.
We collect millions of rows of raw telemetry from our mobile app and want to store it all before filtering.
Game event logs from millions of players need to be stored cheaply for future game design analytics.
We want a single landing zone for data from all our acquisitions before we integrate them properly.
Our journalism team needs a searchable archive of every article, image, and video ever published.
We receive government open data in many formats and want to store it all for public policy research.
Our mobile banking app needs to show the customer's real-time balance from the core banking system.
We want to connect our CRM to our email marketing tool so customer segments sync automatically.
Our checkout system needs to call a payment gateway, a fraud check service, and a loyalty system simultaneously.
Partners should be able to show our product information automatically without our teams sending updates manually.
Our ERP system needs to send purchase orders to supplier systems automatically when stock drops below threshold.
We want third-party fintech apps to access customer account data securely through open banking standards.
Our HR system needs to automatically provision accounts in IT systems when a new employee is hired.
We need to integrate our ticketing system with Slack so support agents get notified instantly of new issues.
Our insurance platform needs to pull vehicle data from government registries to validate claims automatically.
We want to embed a credit score check from a bureau directly into our loan application workflow.
Our accounting software needs to receive invoices from supplier portals automatically without manual entry.
We need to connect our hospital information system to national health registry for automatic patient verification.
Customers should see live delivery options and prices at checkout without staff updating them manually.
We want to push transaction data to our analytics platform the moment a payment is processed.
Our compliance system needs to call a sanctions screening service for every new customer during onboarding.
Our fraud model and credit scoring model both need customer spend history but each team computes it differently.
We have ten ML models in production and each one recomputes the same customer features from scratch.
Features computed in training are calculated differently at inference time, causing model performance to degrade.
We want to reuse engineered features across multiple recommendation models without duplicating computation.
Our data scientists need access to pre-computed, validated features without waiting for engineering to build pipelines.
We need point-in-time correct features for model training to avoid data leakage from the future.
Marketing and risk teams both need customer engagement scores but currently calculate them in different ways.
We want to version our features so we can reproduce model training results from six months ago exactly.
Our churn model needs 200 features and refreshing them all nightly is slowing down our pipelines significantly.
We want a catalogue of all available ML features so teams can discover and reuse them across projects.
Our product recommendation engine needs user behaviour features available in under 10ms at serving time.
We need to ensure that the same feature definitions are used consistently across training and production environments.
Different squads keep rebuilding the same customer lifetime value feature independently wasting engineering time.
Our risk models need historical feature snapshots going back two years for backtesting purposes.
We want to monitor feature drift in production and alert when a feature distribution changes significantly.
Every morning 500 analysts run the same executive dashboard and the database slows to a crawl.
Our product pages load slowly because every visit triggers a fresh database query for prices and stock.
We want to store session tokens and user preferences so login is fast without hitting the database each time.
Our risk calculation engine runs the same Monte Carlo simulations repeatedly with the same inputs.
Our API is getting hammered by repeated requests for the same reference data that almost never changes.
Leaderboard rankings in our game are queried millions of times per hour but only update every five minutes.
We want to pre-compute and cache personalised product recommendations so the homepage loads instantly.
Our search autocomplete is slow because it queries the full product index on every keystroke.
Exchange rates and currency conversion data are queried thousands of times per minute but only change hourly.
We want to materialise complex aggregation queries so reports open in seconds instead of minutes.
Our mobile app makes repeated API calls for the same static configuration data on every screen load.
Frequently accessed customer profile data should be cached in memory to reduce latency for the service desk.
We need to cache the results of regulatory calculation runs so auditors can retrieve them instantly.
Our map application recalculates the same popular routes repeatedly instead of storing precomputed results.
We want to cache aggregated sales metrics so the BI tool does not hit the warehouse on every page refresh.
Our relationship managers open the same customer 360 dashboard hundreds of times a day and the database cannot keep up.
Branch staff need instant access to product rates and terms but every lookup is hitting the core banking system.
Our mobile banking app fetches the same account summary on every login which is causing unnecessary load on backend systems.
Regulatory reference data like country codes and product classifications never change but are queried millions of times daily.
Our fraud scoring API needs customer profile features available in under five milliseconds but the database query takes two seconds.
The same stress test results are retrieved repeatedly by different teams but each request reruns the full calculation.
Our internet banking portal recalculates available credit limit on every page load instead of storing the result.
Exchange rate lookups for currency conversion are hitting our FX system thousands of times per minute with the same requests.
Our probability of default model and our early warning system both need customer financial ratio features but compute them inconsistently.
We want to reuse behavioural features computed for fraud detection in our next best offer recommendation engine.
Our credit scoring model uses different definitions of customer tenure than our churn model causing inconsistent decisions.
We need features calculated at the exact point in time a loan was approved so we can fairly backtest our models.
Our AML transaction monitoring model needs hundreds of aggregated features available within milliseconds at inference time.
Different data science teams keep rebuilding the same customer risk indicators independently wasting months of effort.
We want to detect when input features to our credit model start drifting so we can retrain before performance degrades.
Our regulatory stress testing models need point in time snapshots of customer exposure features for each historical scenario.
Our loan origination system needs to call the credit bureau, land registry and fraud blacklist simultaneously during application.
We want to offer open banking so approved third party apps can access customer account data securely.
Trade documents should be checked automatically against external authorities so staff do not have to do it by hand.
We need to integrate with SWIFT gpi so customers can track their international payments in real time.
Our digital onboarding flow needs to call identity verification, sanctions screening and PEP check services in sequence.
We want to push debit and credit notifications to customers through their preferred channels the moment transactions post.
Our corporate banking portal needs to pull live foreign exchange rates from multiple providers and show the best price.
We need to connect our collateral management system to triparty agents so margin calls are automated end to end.
We want to preserve all raw SWIFT messages, call recordings and chat logs in case regulators request them years later.
Our innovation team wants access to unprocessed customer transaction data to experiment with new fraud detection approaches.
We need to store all document scans from customer onboarding including passports, utility bills and signed contracts.
Our quant team wants years of raw market tick data available for backtesting trading strategies.
We receive unstructured data from news feeds, social media and analyst reports and want to store it all for future NLP work.
All voice recordings from our trading floor need to be archived in original format for compliance purposes.
We want a landing zone where data from every acquired bank is stored as-is before we decide how to integrate it.
Our stress testing team needs raw economic scenario data from multiple providers stored in one accessible place.
Leadership needs one trusted view of the companys total risk exposure.
Customer-facing teams need one complete view of each customer in one place.
The board needs one agreed set of risk numbers for reporting instead of different versions from different teams.
We want branch managers to be able to compare their performance against peers using the same metrics and definitions.
Our product team needs to analyse which customer segments are most profitable across the full product range.
We need historical loan performance data going back ten years to build better credit models.
Our audit committee needs to query any historical transaction or customer record for regulatory investigations.
We want a single repository of customer net promoter scores, complaint history and churn risk for the retention team.
We need to produce the daily general ledger by combining transactions from retail, corporate and investment banking systems every night.
Our SWIFT messages need to be parsed, enriched with customer data, and loaded into our payments database each morning.
We receive account statements from 20 correspondent banks in different formats and need to normalise and load them nightly.
Our credit risk team needs a monthly refresh of customer financial data pulled from bureau, internal systems and market data providers.
We need to extract all transactions above reporting threshold each day and format them for regulatory submission.
Our mortgage system needs nightly synchronisation with the land registry, credit bureau and insurance provider.
We process cheque clearing by batching all images received during the day and running them through our recognition system overnight.
Our finance team needs all branch cash positions consolidated each evening before the next day opens.
We need to block suspicious wire transfers before they leave the bank, not discover them the next morning.
Our anti-money laundering team needs to see transaction patterns forming in real time across multiple accounts.
When a customer's card is used abroad we want to send a push notification before they even leave the terminal.
Our treasury desk needs live exposure calculations as traders execute positions throughout the day.
We want to detect when a customer is going through financial distress by monitoring transaction behaviour as it happens.
Our correspondent banking team needs real-time visibility of nostro account balances as payments flow through.
We need to enforce daily spending limits on corporate cards the moment a transaction is attempted.
Our operations centre needs a live view of all pending settlements to manage intraday liquidity.
Nobody in the bank knows who is responsible for the customer master data and every team maintains their own version.
We cannot answer the regulator's question about where the number in our annual report came from.
Three different departments have three different definitions of what counts as an active customer.
Junior analysts have access to full customer financial data they do not need for their role, which is a compliance risk.
We do not know which systems contain personal data, making it impossible to respond to GDPR subject access requests.
Our data scientists are using a dataset that was deprecated two years ago because nobody told them a new version existed.
There is no agreed definition of default across our credit risk, finance and regulatory reporting teams.
When a senior employee leaves they take all knowledge of what certain database columns mean with them.
We need to prove to the auditor that nobody tampered with the transaction data used in our financial statements.
Our data is shared with third party vendors but we have no visibility of what they do with it after receiving it.
Business users are creating their own spreadsheet copies of database extracts and making decisions from outdated figures.
We have no process for retiring old datasets so the data catalogue is full of tables that no longer exist.
Different countries in our group apply different data retention rules making global compliance reporting impossible.
Our merger has left us with two customer databases and no policy for which one is the authoritative source of truth.
Sensitive credit committee documents are stored on shared drives with no access controls or audit trail.
We cannot tell which version of our risk model was used to make a specific lending decision six months ago.
There is no approval process before new data pipelines go to production so bad data enters our warehouse unchecked.
Our privacy team cannot identify all the places where a customer's date of birth is stored across our systems.
Analysts across the bank are spending hours each week searching for data because there is no central catalogue.
We have no policy on how long raw transaction logs should be kept so some regions delete them after 90 days and others never do.
Our cloud migration revealed that nobody documented which legacy tables were still in active use and which were abandoned.
Two business units are collecting the same customer consent information in different formats making consolidation impossible.
We failed a model risk review because we could not demonstrate the data used to train the model was fit for purpose.
There is no process for business users to flag when data looks wrong so errors go unnoticed for months.
Our data sharing agreements with partners are not documented anywhere making legal review extremely difficult.
Our Basel III reports show different risk weighted assets depending on which system produces the number.
Customer due diligence records are incomplete across our systems making KYC refresh extremely difficult.
We have duplicate customer records across our retail and private banking systems with no way to link them.
Our transaction monitoring system is generating too many false positives because the underlying customer data is unreliable.
Interest calculations are wrong because the same product has different rate definitions in different systems.
We failed a regulatory audit because we could not demonstrate the lineage of figures in our liquidity coverage ratio report.
Account opening data is missing mandatory fields because branches are finding workarounds in the onboarding system.
Our credit decisions are being challenged because the income data used in scoring comes from an unvalidated source.
Our reports show different customer counts depending on which system you query and nobody knows which is correct.
We discovered that 30% of our customer email addresses are invalid and our campaigns are failing because of it.
Regulators are asking us to prove the lineage of every number in our capital adequacy report.
Our data scientists complain they spend 80% of their time cleaning data before they can do any analysis.
We have no idea who owns which dataset, and people keep using outdated versions of the same table.
Product prices in our database have duplicates and inconsistencies that cause wrong charges to customers.
We need to ensure patient records are complete and consistent before they are shared with insurance providers.
Our ML model performance is degrading because the input data has started drifting from what it was trained on.
We need automated checks that validate incoming data files before they are loaded into production systems.
Nobody in our organisation knows what the column named cust_ref_id actually means or where it comes from.
We need a data catalogue so analysts can discover and understand available datasets without asking engineers.
Our GDPR compliance requires us to track exactly where personal data is stored and who has accessed it.
Supplier codes are entered differently across regions making it impossible to get a global view of spend.
We need master data management to ensure one consistent definition of a customer across all systems.
Test data in our development environment contains real customer personal data which is a compliance risk.

Our global cloud platform re-evaluates the same IAM policy rules for the same users and resources on every API request.

We cannot prove which data version was used to support an old drug approval decision.

The intelligence agency has no process to ensure analytical products are based on the most current and authorised data sources.
Our national statistics office publishes figures that different government departments dispute because there is no single authoritative data source.

The national tax authority cannot trace which rules version was applied to a taxpayer assessment made three years ago when it is challenged in court.
Patient records are spread across merged systems and nobody knows which one should be treated as the main record.
The ministry of justice has no data sharing agreement between police, prosecution and courts so case data is re-entered manually at every handoff.
